data.title: Europe faces incremental defence shift amid geopolitical pressures
data.description:
Investing.com - Europe is likely to take incremental steps rather than a transformational approach in adjusting its defence strategy, Barclays (LON:BARC) said amid the mounting geopolitical pressures and inefficiencies in defence spending.
Russian aggression in Ukraine, more assertive U.S. trade and foreign policies, and rising competition from China have intensified the need for a re-evaluation of European security policies. However, it expects changes to be gradual.
Increasing euro area defence spending from about 2% to 3.5% of GDP by 2035 could boost euro area GDP by 1.6 percentage points compared to a no-increase scenario, Barclays said. It estimated this would require approximately €1.63 trillion in additional defence spending, with €680 billion allocated to investment.
The bank noted that national-level decision-making has led to inefficiencies, with spending priorities varying across EU countries. While discussions on joint defence initiatives are underway, Barclays does not expect a large-scale EU-level funding facility similar to the bloc’s post-pandemic recovery fund.
Instead, the European Investment Bank could play a greater role in financing security investments, and some EU and non-EU countries may establish a special purpose vehicle to issue bonds for defence-related industries.
Barclays said any significant increase in defence spending would have fiscal consequences, requiring flexible application of EU fiscal rules, potential reallocation of unused EU funds, and negotiations for the bloc’s 2028-2034 budget framework.
Well-designed pan-European solution that boosts spending, reallocates resources, and promotes research and development would yield better results than national-level increases, Barclays added.

data.title: Analyst explains how “tariffs” affect asset prices
data.description:
Investing.com - In a note this week, Piper Sandler analysts examined the impact of tariffs on asset prices, concluding that while tariffs may prompt flights to quality, the broader picture suggests that economic policy uncertainty (EPU) plays a larger role in shaping market reactions.
Their analysis highlights that previous tariff announcements during the first Trump administration led to U.S. Treasury (UST) rallies and equity declines, with an 11.5% cumulative drop in stocks following 11 tariff events between 2018 and 2019. 
However, they argue that these reactions are not necessarily consistent, and their updated research challenges the assumption that tariffs alone drive market behavior. Instead, they find that EPU shocks, rather than just trade policy uncertainty (TPU), generate more pronounced flight-to-quality (FTQ) responses. 
"The responses of USTs and the S&P suggest that overall policy uncertainty tends to nudge yields and equities lower, not tariffs per se," the analysts state.
Piper Sandler also warns against making investment decisions based on intuition rather than systematic analysis. 
"Just because numeracy is hard to come by doesn’t make storytelling more promising or less risky," they note, cautioning against speculative "if A then B then C" trades that rely on uncertain assumptions. 
Instead, they advocate for structured portfolio approaches that minimize exposure to policy uncertainty.
In their research, the firm attempted to construct a diversified portfolio across 29 global assets that reduces sensitivity to both TPU and EPU. 
The key takeaway for investors, according to the analysts, is to focus less on individual tariff announcements and more on the broader uncertainty surrounding fiscal, monetary, and trade policies.

data.title: Who's a bigger customer for TSMC – Nvidia or Apple?
data.description:
Investing.com - Nvidia is set to become as large a customer as Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co this year, as the chipmaker’s AI-driven growth accelerates.
Apple is not shrinking in importance, but Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is expanding rapidly. Bernstein estimates Nvidia’s contribution to TSMC’s revenue will grow from 5-10% in 2023 to the low-20s% in 2025, matching Apple’s share.
TSMC’s AI and backend business, particularly advanced packaging, are key growth drivers, the brokerage said, forecasting that AI will account for more than 20% of TSMC’s total revenue this year. The company is expected to navigate changes in production schedules across Nvidia's Blackwell, Blackwell Ultra, and Rubin chips while maintaining full capacity.
Bernstein projects TSMC’s earnings per share to grow 40% this year, with data center AI revenue rising from 6% of total revenue in 2023 to 14% in 2024 and over 20% in 2025.
While Nvidia's GPU business remains dominant, with GPUs expected to make up two-thirds of TSMC’s AI-related demand this year, the rise of ASIC chips—led by Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Google (NASDAQ:GOOGL) is a long-term trend.
Bernstein highlighted TSMC’s growing presence in backend services, predicting it will surpass ASE Technology Holding Co (NYSE:ASX) Ltd to become the world’s largest outsourced semiconductor assembly and test provider this year.
The firm maintained an "Outperform" rating on TSMC, setting a one-year price target of NT$1,430, implying a 30% upside.

data.title: Street Calls of the Week
data.description:
Investing.com - Here is your Pro Recap of the top takeaways from Wall Street analysts for the past week.
InvestingPro subscribers always get first dibs on market-moving AI analyst comments. Upgrade today!
Monday - US Markets Closed for George Washington’s Birthday
Bath & Body Works
What happened? On Tuesday, JPMorgan upgraded Bath & Body Works Inc. (NYSE:BBWI) to Overweight with a price target of $47.
*TLDR: BBWI foresees revenue growth, margin expansion. Cash flow boosts shareholder returns.
What’s the full story? JPMorgan highlights three key drivers behind its upgraded outlook for BBWI:
First, the bank models a revenue inflection to +3% growth in FY25, reversing a -2.5% decline over the past three years. Post-pandemic headwinds in candles and sanitizers, once a 300bps drag, are now immaterial. Low-single-digit square footage growth and innovation in core categories like home fragrance and body care are expected to drive growth, while adjacent segments like hair and men’s products could contribute ~100bps.
Second, the investment cycle is shifting, with operating margins set to expand by +10-15bps in FY25. Gross margin improvements and SG&A leverage, supported by stabilized marketing and technology costs, are key catalysts. JPMorgan sees BBWI’s margins bridging toward its long-term target of ~20%, driven by fixed-cost leverage and a multi-year growth trajectory. The bank notes BBWI’s margins rank among the top 5 in its coverage universe.
Finally, BBWI’s free cash flow generation of $825M+ over FY25/26 supports a visible low-teens total shareholder return profile. A balanced capital allocation strategy includes $625M in annual buybacks (driving ~5% EPS growth) and ~$200M in dividends (~2% yield). Debt leverage, held steady at 2.5x, could unlock an additional $480M in buybacks. Combined, JPMorgan projects 9% annual returns from capital allocation alone, fueling a robust 13-14% total shareholder return outlook.
NetApp
What happened? On Wednesday, BofA Securities upgraded NetApp Inc (NASDAQ:NTAP) to Neutral with a $128 price target.
*TLDR: Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) harbors favorable view on AI-driven growth and stable margins. Limited upside keeps the outlook cautious.
What’s the full story? Morgan Stanley analysts highlight several catalysts driving the revision: expanding total addressable market fueled by AI and public cloud demand; a rebound in macroeconomic conditions and all-flash-array market share gains; stable gross margins in the 71-72% range following robust F24 margin expansion; operating margin growth toward long-term targets above 30%; and strong free cash flow margins supporting capital returns and EPS outperformance. While these trends signal improving fundamentals, limited upside to consensus estimates keeps the rating at Neutral.
NTAP’s F24 free cash flow margin reached 24%, with a long-term target of 20% through FY27. The company has historically returned nearly 100% of free cash flow to shareholders. Morgan Stanley models EPS growth in line with consensus estimates—a 7% CAGR through FY27—though both the firm and Bank of America project F26 and F27 EPS below NTAP’s double-digit annual growth target.
While the outlook is steadier, the analysts remain cautious on substantial upward re-rating potential.
NXP Semiconductors
What happened? On Thursday, Citi upgraded NXP Semiconductors NV (NASDAQ:NXPI) to Buy with a $290 price target.
*TLDR: Analog recovery nears as Industrial market rebounds.
What’s the full story? The analog recovery is on the horizon, with ADI signaling a broad-based resurgence in the Industrial end market and guiding for normal seasonality amid rising bookings. While Citi anticipates continued weakness in the Automotive sector—which accounts for 58% of 4Q24 sales—the analysts expect the rebound in Industrial markets (17% of 4Q24 sales) to partially offset the drag, leading to only a modest cut to estimates. This shift underscores a pivotal inflection point in the analog cycle.
NXPI’s stock, buoyed by Infineon (OTC:IFNNY) and ADI’s upbeat earnings commentary, has surged in recent weeks, reflecting early signs of recovery. The analysts project NXPI’s multiple to expand by 30-40% during this upturn, nearing the lower end of its historical 30-100% range. With consensus estimates down 30% from their peak and now only 9% above Citi’s forecasts, the worst appears to be over.
Citi upgrades NXPI to Buy, raising the price target to $290 (27x C26 EPS) from $210 (20x C26 EPS), driven by multiple expansion and the analog replenishment cycle. The analysts see significant upside as the recovery gains momentum.
Akamai Technologies
What happened? On Friday, Piper downgraded Akamai Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:AKAM) to Neutral with a $100 price target.
*TLDR: Piper downgrades Akamai citing weak 2025 outlook and murky growth prospects.
What’s the full story? Piper downgrades Akamai to Neutral after Q4 results reveal a 2025 outlook that falls short of even cautious expectations, compounded by a newly introduced 3-5-year framework. While the brokerage acknowledges progress in Enterprise IaaS, go-to-market adjustments, and stabilized operations excluding TikTok, significant challenges loom. Low growth, including in core Security, declining margins, higher capex needs, and uncertainties around revenue substitution create a murky path forward. Fast-growing segments like ZTNA, IaaS, and API remain marginal as the core business decelerates, prompting Piper to advise investors to wait for clarity before re-engaging.
The brokerage also questions the optimism of the 3-5-year framework, raising concerns it echoes unmet targets from prior analyst days. With estimates revised downward, Piper cuts its price target to $100, reflecting tempered expectations. Until Akamai navigates these complexities and delivers more consistent results, Piper remains on the sidelines.

data.title: Russia launches war's largest drone attack on Ukraine, Kyiv says
data.description: KYIV (Reuters) - Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Sunday that Russia launched more than 200 drones in an overnight attack, the largest of the war, as he condemned Russia's "aerial terror" and called for unity among Ukraine's allies. "Every day, our people stand against aerial terror," he wrote on X. "On the eve of the third anniversary of the full-scale war, Russia launched 267 attack drones against Ukraine — the largest attack since Iranian drones began striking Ukrainian cities and villages." Ukraine's air force said in a statement that 138 of the drones were downed and another 119 disappeared from radars after being jammed by electronic warfare, adding that Russia also launched three ballistic missiles. Damage was reported in five regions of Ukraine. Moscow has launched near-nightly massed drone attacks at Ukraine for months, seeking to exhaust air defences. Zelenskiy said that in total, nearly 1,150 attack drones, more than 1,400 guided aerial bombs and 35 missiles of various types, were launched by Russia at Ukraine in the past week. The president thanked those operating Ukraine's air defences, and called on the country's foreign allies to stand united to secure a "just and lasting peace". "This is achievable through the unity of all partners — we need the strength of all of Europe, the strength of America, the strength of everyone who seeks lasting peace." Kyiv and its European allies have been unnerved by new U.S. President Donald Trump verbally attacking Zelenskiy last week, as well as by a meeting between U.S. and Russian delegations in Riyadh to which Ukraine was not invited.

data.title: Trump gets middling grades on Americans' top issues, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds
data.description: By Jason Lange and Bo Erickson WASHINGTON (Reuters) - Americans give President Donald Trump middling marks on his handling of the economy and efforts to shrink the government and are unimpressed by some of the early fights he has picked, such as proposals to take over Gaza, a Reuters/Ipsos poll shows. The poll, conducted February 13-18, asked more than 4,000 U.S. adults nationwide whether they supported a range of positions staked out by Trump and how much the issues would motivate them to vote in the future. The results point to Trump putting considerable effort into policies that many Americans don't like, or don't consider very important. A wave of frustration over prolonged inflation helped power Trump to victory in November, and a majority of respondents - 58% - said inflation would be a major factor in deciding their vote in future elections. But just 32% approved of the job Trump was doing on inflation. Views of the economy by households deteriorated this month to the lowest level in over a year, according to a widely followed survey by the University of Michigan. Just 25% of respondents - and only half of Republicans - said they supported Trump's idea of having the U.S. government take over Gaza and resettle Palestinians elsewhere.  "I thought that was a moronic idea because it's infeasible," Willard Moore, a Republican lawyer in New York City who participated in the poll, referring to the Gaza proposal. "If you did it, it would cost a lot of money, and at the end what would you have, some sort of resort? Like, what good is that for anyone?" A notable portion of Trump's 2024 voters broke with some of the president's early actions and ideas. About a third of Trump voters opposed the proposal to end birthright citizenship and one in five opposed his administration's move to end diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives. Americans do consider Trump's push to downsize government important, but are divided mostly along party lines on whether they support it. Sixty percent of respondents said the so-called Department of Government Efficiency task force for cutting federal spending, which is led by Elon Musk, would influence their vote in the next federal elections - 2026, when Democrats will seek to win back control of Congress. But only 42% of the country supports the endeavor and 53% oppose it. "He's just rushing a little bit. I think the whole thing with the DOGE is being rushed a little bit," said Gerald Dunn, a Republican 66-year-old martial arts instructor from Staatsburg, in New York State's Hudson (NYSE:HUD) Valley. "I like what he is doing but I think a lot of what he says is just BS. When he starts talking about annexing Greenland and annexing Canada, you know that's just smoke." EDUCATION WORRY Trump's call to abolish the Department of Education - a move that would require Congress' support - met with broad opposition, with 65% of respondents overall and four in ten Republicans opposed. "I have a child who is on the spectrum, and I have the luxury to provide for her to go to school for children who have autism. If that was taken away, I wouldn’t know what I would do," said Mikeriah Perry, a 25-year-old from Raleigh, North Carolina, who said she leans Democratic. "I wouldn’t have the proper resources to assist my child to be the best person they can be when it is time for them to go to the general public schools." Musk's cost-cutting, however, is wildly popular among Trump's hardline supporters - those in the survey who said they strongly identify with the president's Make America Great Again, or MAGA, movement. Ninety-four percent of MAGA followers back the Musk-led effort and 78% said it would be a "very motivating" or "motivating" for them in future elections. The same share of core MAGA voters consider increasing the deportation of illegal immigrants to be a big motivator for voting. It's an issue that was at the center of Trump's presidential campaign last year. Across all respondents in the Reuters/Ipsos poll, just over half - 55% - supported increasing deportations, compared to 41% who were opposed. But Trump's performance on immigration was approved by less than half of respondents - 47%. Trump also got mixed marks on his proposed tariffs, particularly on Canada. Fifty-nine percent of respondents, including a quarter of Republicans, rejected this idea. "I struggle with things like picking a fight with Canada. Why are we picking a fight with Canada?" said Todd Wellman, a 49-year-old Republican from Indianapolis, who said he wrote in now-Vice President JD Vance as his choice for president in November. Regardless of his doubts about Trump, he said he preferred him to Democratic predecessor Joe Biden and added of Trump: "I support the direction in which he's trying to take us, but I don't always support his methods or path in getting us there." The poll surveyed 4,145 U.S. adults nationwide and had a margin of error of 2 percentage points.

data.title: Zelenskiy says he is willing to give up presidency if it means peace in Ukraine
data.description: KYIV (Reuters) - Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Sunday he was willing to give up his position if it meant peace in Ukraine, quipping that he could exchange his departure for Ukraine's entry into NATO. Zelenskiy also said he wanted to see U.S. President Donald Trump as a partner to Ukraine and more than a simply a mediator between Kyiv and Moscow. "I really want it to be more than just mediation... that's not enough," he told a press conference in Kyiv.

data.title: China to lay out ambitious growth plan at NPC with stimulus playing leading role
data.description:
Investing.com - The upcoming National People’s Congress meeting is likely to see Beijing roll out ambitious economic growth targets underpinned by a flurry of fiscal and monetary liquidity.     
We expect the upcoming National People’s Congress meeting (NPC) starting on March 5 to follow the policy stances set in the Central Economic Work Conference (CEWC) last December and set “around 5%” GDP growth target again to anchor expectations," UBS economists said in a note. 
This target, however, "looks ambitious given the lingering growth headwinds and uncertainties," the economists said, maintaining their baseline forecast of GDP growth at around 4% in 2025.
The NPC meeting is likely to prioritize “stabilizing growth” as the central task and emphasize boosting domestic demand with more supportive macro policies, with “reviving household consumption” listed as a top task, they added.
Still to achieve this 5% target, Beijing is likely to lay out a plan that leans on fiscal policy, the economists said, projecting headline budget deficit of around 4% of GDP, which would be highest on record, above the initial target of 3% of GDP for 2024. 
This expected increase in the budget deficit will likely prompt a ramp up in government bond sales. The likely acceleration of government bond issues will put the spotlight on the People's Bank of China to persist with accommodative monetary policy measures to ensure ample liquidity. 
"We expect the PBC to cut policy rates by 30-40bp in 2025 likely in later months after NPC meeting, and commercial banks to lower deposit rates further," UBS said.
U.S.-China trade relations are also expected to sway policy at a time when expect the 10% additional tariffs that the U.S. on all imports from China - related to Fentanyl control - merely represent the opening salvo from the Donald Trump administration.  
"We think the US administration may roll out more additional tariffs on Chinese goods in stages after its ongoing trade policy review concludes by April 1," UBS said. "Thus we expect China’s government to roll out additional policy support on top of NPC measures throughout 2025 in the case of more significant external shocks," it added.

data.title: China announces rural revitalisation plans in annual policy blueprint
data.description: SHENZHEN, China (Reuters) - China will deepen its rural reforms as part of efforts to revitalise the agricultural sector and bolster food security in the face of U.S. tariffs, an economic slowdown and climate change, state media reported on Sunday, citing a rural policy document. The State Council's annual rural policy blueprint, known as the "No.1 document", outlined plans to improve rewards and subsidy systems for major grain-producing areas, promoting the industrialisation of biotech cultivation among other measures, state news agency Xinhua reported. Grain production in the world's top grain importer reached a record high of 706.5 million metric tons last year, up 1.6% from 2023. The latest policy guideline sharpens China's focus on self-sufficiency and supply stability to counter potential disruptions to agricultural trade with the United States, the European Union and Canada. While approval has been granted for GM and gene-edited soybeans, wheat and corn, planting remains limited and closely controlled, with progress and scale of cultivation unclear. The document said China would monitor and regulate pig production capacity and support the beef and dairy cattle industries while encouraging financial institutions to increase funding for rural revitalisation.

data.title: Cloud and communication stocks earnings preview
data.description: Oppenheimer analysts have issued Q4 earnings previews for cloud and communication stocks including Zoom Communications (NASDAQ:ZM), Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) and Ring Central (RNG).Discussing Zoom Communications Inc., analysts expect a solid 4Q25 as the company will be able to raise prices while building momentum with its new AI platform. 2026 guidance and impact to top-line growth from new go-to market movements will be the focus.Furthermore, margins could see some pressure as the company will be likely investing in AI and investors will also be looking at the overall adoption and usage of its AI companion.Analysts would like to see more color on CCaaS seat growth and cross-sell trends though it is still early days. The report states that analysts believe ZM could beat the Street’s expectation of 3% growth next year and might likely be above 4%.While ZM has historically maintained stable prices, it has increased monthly rates by 5-10% for SMB plans mid-4Q. This will likely add 2% to annual revenue growth though it will likely take two to three years for this growth to flow through.ZM is Perform-rated at Oppenheimer.Analysts believe that ZM: “(1) has a differentiated and scalable communications platform that improves team productivity and collaboration; (2) can gain share within the meeting solutions and broader unified communications TAM; and (3) has a mutually beneficial land-and-expand model combining self-service adoption (free and paid) with an emerging top-down enterprises sales motion (which can drive expansion in >$100,000 customers).”Oppenheimer also recently upgraded to Akamai Technologies, Inc. to Outperform from Perform based on stronger cloud compute growth.Analysts point out that in 4Q AKAM saw channel checks and CDN volumes/pricing improvements. The company is undertaking a change from CDN/security focus to cloud computing, which is almost 20% of revenue and growing more than 20%.The report states that the cloud and edge computing platform buildout at AKAM will benefit from low latency AI inferencing. However, according to channel checks this is progressing slower than expected. Analysts expect the company to guide 8% revenue growth compared with the Street’s 7%. Also, Oppenheimer observes that the Edgio acquisition might be driving AKAM to guide above the Street for the first time in five years.According to analysts’ upside scenario, “AKAM improves its services and enables all four services over upgraded infrastructure; Pricing competition is less than expected, and industry players remain rational; and, Enterprise security products see higher attach rates and drive higher than expected top-line growth.”Finally, RingCentral (NYSE:RNG) Inc is Perform-rated at Oppenheimer. Analysts are expecting a solid 4Q following a beat and a raise last quarter.“RNG has beat the midpoint of its quarterly revenue guidance by over 100bps for the past three consecutive quarters,” analysts point out.Analysts believe this feat will likely be repeated in 4Q. Although the company is seeing enterprise resistance to adoption of GenAI integration as UCaaS and CCaaS sectors are converging, it is still a big focus for RNG and a competitive necessity, says the report.Analysts also highlight that the above trend demand increase created by the pandemic has reverted and competition between players in the sector, like Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Zoom has heated up. Furthermore, the communication sector is becoming more bundled with applications as the workplace becomes more mobile virtualized.According to analysts’ upside scenario for RNG, “Subscriptions revenue return to 15%-plus: Shows a faster pace of margin expansion; Rapid improvements to customer lifetime values; Faster market share gains.”
